summary: Matched Sysdolphin random by replacing asm/random.o with src/sysdolphin/random.o, adding random.c/random.h, naming HSD_Rand, HSD_Randf, HSD_Randi, and _HSD_RandForgetMemory, and propagating the new symbol names through many asm call sites. The PR body says HSD_Rand and _HSD_RandForgetMemory matched, while the other two functions did not match the GNT4 reference, so HSD_Randf and HSD_Randi were kept as inline assembly under matching builds with NON_MATCHING C alternatives. It also fixed the initialize.h include guard to match existing header-guard style.
what_changed: Added src/sysdolphin/random.c with HSD_Rand, HSD_Randf, HSD_Randi, and _HSD_RandForgetMemory.
Added src/sysdolphin/random.h declaring HSD_Rand, HSD_Randf, HSD_Randi, and _HSD_RandForgetMemory.
Removed asm/random.s from the build by changing obj_files.mk from $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/random.o to $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/random.o.
Renamed widespread asm branch targets from func_80380528 to HSD_Randf and from func_80380580 to HSD_Randi across fighters, stages, bytecode, text asm, and other files.
Changed src/sysdolphin/initialize.h header guard from _initialize_ to _initialize_h_.
Implemented HSD_Rand in C as a linear congruential generator update using multiplier 214013 and increment 2531011, returning the upper 16 bits of the seed.
Implemented _HSD_RandForgetMemory to reset lbl_804D5F94 to &lbl_804D5F90 when the current seed pointer falls inside the supplied memory range.
smart_moves: Split matched and non-matching work explicitly: HSD_Rand and _HSD_RandForgetMemory were moved to C, while HSD_Randf and HSD_Randi retained exact inline assembly for matching with NON_MATCHING C versions kept as documentation.
Used the new real function names to relabel existing asm call sites, making future decompilation of unrelated fighters, stages, and scripts more readable without changing machine code.
Kept random.h small and direct, exposing only the four identified random API functions.
Updated obj_files.mk to swap the object file in place, preserving the existing object order while replacing asm with C.
Fixed initialize.h guard naming while touching Sysdolphin headers, preventing inconsistency with the apparent _name_h_ convention.
decomp_lessons: When a source file is partially matchable, it can still replace an asm object if non-matching functions are guarded with inline asm in the matching path and C pseudocode under NON_MATCHING.
Large asm churn in a matching PR may be symbol propagation rather than code changes; here many one-line hunks only rename calls to HSD_Randf or HSD_Randi.
External data labels can be documented with comments while still using unresolved label names, as seen with lbl_804D5F90 as seed and lbl_804D5F94 as seed_ptr.
Do not force suspected library-reference implementations if they do not match the target binary; the PR body explicitly notes that the other two functions did not appear to match what GNT4 had.
Header guard cleanup is valuable in decomp work because inconsistent guards can obscure include dependencies and naming conventions.
naming_conventions: Sysdolphin random API uses HSD_ prefix: HSD_Rand, HSD_Randf, HSD_Randi.
Internal memory-forget hook keeps a leading underscore: _HSD_RandForgetMemory.
Header guards use lowercase file-name style with leading/trailing underscores, e.g. _random_h_ and _initialize_h_.
Unidentified globals remain lbl_ addresses but may include clarifying comments, e.g. lbl_804D5F90 // seed and lbl_804D5F94 // seed_ptr.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline asm for HSD_Randf and HSD_Randi in the matching build because the C versions under NON_MATCHING did not match.
Wrapped _HSD_RandForgetMemory with #pragma push, #pragma force_active on, #pragma peephole on, and #pragma pop, indicating attention to linker/codegen retention and peephole behavior.
Preserved exact branch instruction encodings while renaming call targets in asm, e.g. bl func_80380528 became bl HSD_Randf and bl func_80380580 became bl HSD_Randi.
Replaced one asm object with one source object in the same object-list position to keep layout stable.
Used nofralloc inline asm blocks for leaf/random functions that needed exact prologue or no-prologue behavior.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The only explanatory body text says HSD_Rand and _HSD_RandForgetMemory matched, while HSD_Randf and HSD_Randi did not appear to match the GNT4 reference, and that initialize.h was fixed to match existing defines.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match HSD_Randf or HSD_Randi without inline asm.
Search for remaining calls to func_80380528 or func_80380580 after PR 59.
Check whether lbl_804D5F90 and lbl_804D5F94 were later renamed to seed and seed_ptr or equivalent.
Look for Sysdolphin header guard cleanups using the _name_h_ convention.
Find other PRs using NON_MATCHING C alternatives beside inline asm to replace an asm object.
